tweets.text:
the way to make memecoins fair is to disallow bundling by being overly transparent w/ the supply and allowing market participants to earn their supply through recurring distributions based on marketing and activation milestones

you create community from the ground up while getting rid of the risks of anonymous bundlers nuking supply on peoples heads while also efficiently distributing value to the right market participants

earning through bagworking

thread_tweets.text:
The 3% is the total you can lose in a 24h window, based on your closed balance, and it resets daily. 

So if you're down 1.5-2%, you stop, wait for the reset, and you can trade again with a fresh 3% daily DD. 

What you're describing is full-porting your whole account at 5x into one position. 

The daily drawdown exists to discourage exactly that. We'll let you do it, it's just not the optimal way to trade prop.

Most people using prop firms get this. They risk 0.5-1% per trade because they don't need to full-port max leverage. The whole point of prop is you're already working with a bigger account than you'd have on your own.

And to give you a comp outside of prop. A $1,000 account at 100x gets liqd on a 1% move.

A $100k account at Breakout costs $330-$800 depending on the eval. That fee is your entire max downside.

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

US successfully escorted one group of tankers in through the Oman lane.

All of them had their AIS off, but there was one Indian cargo ship that had it on when it went through. The list can be seen below.

Following two days of US strikes, the US is back to escorting ships in. My guess is that it will try to escort a group out tonight, and Iran can either respond and create more escalation going into the weekend or it can lose leverage.

The Oman lane will be heavily contested in the coming days, which leaves either side with no choice but to escalate.

I have included the video from @MarineTraffic.

tweets.text:
My other takeaway from the 2040ai version a path, is that it says Americans will have ubi at much higher rates than the rest of the planet ($1 mil per U.S. adult) and then giving something like $15k to some allies in other countries.

So in this scenario means that:
1. US citizens will be top 5% globally
2. Illegal immigration will become an even bigger hot topic bc the chasm between the haves (Americans) and have nots (non Americans ex China) will start to become so wide with such high stakes.  Need to get our house in order faster.
